full_scripture: Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Creation mandate given to humanity.

full_scripture: Subdue the earth.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Humanity receives delegated dominion responsibility.

full_scripture: Have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Dominion command connected to humanity's representative authority.

full_scripture: Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed... to you it shall be for meat.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Primarily declarative provision statement rather than direct command.

full_scripture: To every beast of the earth... every green herb for meat.
speaker: God
command_summary: Green plants assigned for creature sustenance
interpretation_notes: Describes created order and provision structure.

full_scripture: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Mandate of stewardship over Eden; implied vocational governance.

full_scripture: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Permission statement rather than strict imperative.

full_scripture: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.
speaker: God
conditions: if eating from tree of knowledge
command_summary: Do not eat from the tree of knowledge
interpretation_notes: Explicit prohibition tied to covenantal obedience and consequence.

full_scripture: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Not a direct command but foundational relational principle.

full_scripture: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Leave parents and unite with spouse
interpretation_notes: Foundational marriage principle later quoted directly by Jesus and Paul.

full_scripture: Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?
speaker: Serpent
interpretation_notes: Serpent frames God's command as a misleading question.

full_scripture: But of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.
speaker: Eve
command_summary: Do not eat from the forbidden tree
interpretation_notes: Eve repeats God's prohibition from Genesis 2.

full_scripture: Ye shall not surely die:
speaker: Serpent
interpretation_notes: Direct contradiction of divine warning.

full_scripture: Upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Judgment pronounced upon the serpent.

full_scripture: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed.
speaker: God
command_summary: Enmity established between serpent and woman
interpretation_notes: Traditionally interpreted as protoevangelium; tagged conservatively.

full_scripture: I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Judgment consequence following the fall.

full_scripture: Cursed is the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life;
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Creation affected by human disobedience.

full_scripture: If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: The command is embedded within a conditional warning statement directed to Cain.

full_scripture: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth unto me from the ground.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Not a command, but a judicial rebuke and confrontation.

full_scripture: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand; When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
speaker: God
command_summary: Cain is cursed from the earth
interpretation_notes: Divine judicial curse pronounced after Cain murders Abel.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the LORD set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.
speaker: God
command_summary: Vengeance pronounced against anyone who kills Cain
interpretation_notes: Implicit prohibition through divine warning rather than direct imperative.

full_scripture: In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Humanity created in the likeness of God
interpretation_notes: Restates creation theology from Genesis 1 with genealogical framing.

full_scripture: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his image;
speaker: Moses
interpretation_notes: Echoes Genesis 1 image-language but now mediated through fallen humanity.

full_scripture: And Enoch walked with God... and he was not; for God took him.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Enoch walked with God and was taken by God
interpretation_notes: Exceptional interruption in genealogy emphasizing covenant faithfulness and divine favor.

full_scripture: And he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands.
speaker: Lamech
command_summary: Noah associated with future comfort from cursed labor
interpretation_notes: Connects Noah typologically to relief from Genesis 3 curse language.

full_scripture: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them.
speaker: God
command_summary: The earth is filled with violence
interpretation_notes: Divine declaration establishing moral corruption before flood judgment.

full_scripture: and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct declaration of impending flood judgment.

full_scripture: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark.
speaker: God
command_summary: Build an ark of gopher wood
interpretation_notes: Primary divine command initiating ark construction.

full_scripture: and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Secondary construction instruction expanding ark specifications.

full_scripture: And this is the fashion which thou shalt make it of: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits.
speaker: God
command_summary: Construct the ark according to specified dimensions
interpretation_notes: Detailed dimensional instructions for ark construction.

full_scripture: A window shalt thou make to the ark.
speaker: God
command_summary: Add a window to the ark
interpretation_notes: Specific ark design instruction.

full_scripture: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring living creatures into the ark
interpretation_notes: Preservation command concerning animal life.

full_scripture: And take thou unto thee of all food that is eaten, and thou shalt gather it to thee.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Provisioning instruction connected to flood preparation.

full_scripture: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.
speaker: Narrator
interpretation_notes: Narrative affirmation of Noah's obedience.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Noah, Come thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct divine command to Noah and household.

full_scripture: Of every clean beast thou shalt take to thee by sevens, the male and his female...
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Expands prior ark-animal instructions with clean/unclean distinction.

full_scripture: For yet seven days, and I will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights...
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Divine declaration of imminent flood judgment.

full_scripture: And Noah did according unto all that the LORD commanded him.
speaker: Moses
interpretation_notes: Narrative obedience statement rather than command itself.

full_scripture: In the selfsame day entered Noah, and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth... into the ark.
speaker: Moses
interpretation_notes: Narrative fulfillment of prior divine command.

full_scripture: and the LORD shut him in.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: God sealed Noah in the ark
interpretation_notes: Symbolic act of divine protection and preservation.

full_scripture: And God spake unto Noah, saying, Go forth of the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons' wives with thee. Bring forth with thee every living thing that is with thee...
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Primary migration command concluding flood preservation narrative.

full_scripture: Bring forth with thee every living thing that is with thee...
speaker: God
command_summary: Release living creatures from the ark
interpretation_notes: Extends dominion/stewardship responsibilities after flood preservation.

full_scripture: And Noah builded an altar unto the LORD; and took of every clean beast...
speaker: Noah
command_summary: Offer burnt offerings to the LORD
interpretation_notes: Not a direct command but treated as inferred worship principle through narrative example.

full_scripture: And the LORD said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man's sake...
speaker: God
command_summary: God promises stability of creation order
interpretation_notes: Universal covenant stability declaration following sacrificial worship.

full_scripture: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct continuation of creation mandate language from Genesis 1.

full_scripture: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Continuation of humanity’s post-flood repopulation mandate.

full_scripture: Every moving thing that liveth shall be meat for you; even as the green herb have I given you all things.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Marks dietary expansion beyond Genesis 1 vegetation allowance.

full_scripture: But flesh with the life thereof, which is the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Foundational blood prohibition later repeated in Mosaic law and Acts 15.

full_scripture: And surely your blood of your lives will I require... Whoso sheddeth man's blood, by man shall his blood be shed.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Establishes post-flood judicial principle grounded in imago Dei.

full_scripture: And you, be ye fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Restates creation and post-flood multiplication mandate.

full_scripture: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly.
speaker: People of Shinar
command_summary: Make bricks and bake them thoroughly
interpretation_notes: Collective human imperative related to construction and unified civilization.

full_scripture: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven.
speaker: People of Shinar
interpretation_notes: Unified human construction project associated with Babel narrative.

full_scripture: and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.
speaker: People of Shinar
interpretation_notes: Imperative tied to collective identity and resistance to dispersion mandate.

full_scripture: and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Divine observation preceding Babel judgment.

full_scripture: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another's speech.
speaker: God
conditions: that they may not understand one another
interpretation_notes: Divine intervention causing linguistic division and dispersion.

full_scripture: So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Humanity was scattered across the earth
interpretation_notes: Narrative result of Babel judgment and dispersion.

full_scripture: Now the LORD had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father's house, unto a land that I will shew thee:
speaker: God
conditions: unto a land that I will shew thee
interpretation_notes: Foundational Abrahamic call narrative.

full_scripture: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing:
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Declarative covenant promise with implied missional function.

full_scripture: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee:
speaker: God
conditions: those who bless or curse Abram
command_summary: Blessing and cursing tied to Abram
interpretation_notes: Covenantal blessing/curse structure.

full_scripture: and there he builded an altar unto the LORD, and called upon the name of the LORD.
speaker: Narrator
interpretation_notes: Narrative worship action treated as inferred worship principle.

full_scripture: Let there be no strife, I pray thee, between me and thee, and between my herdmen and thy herdmen; for we be brethren.
speaker: Abram
command_summary: Do not strive with one another
interpretation_notes: Explicit relational appeal toward peace and kinship.

full_scripture: Is not the whole land before thee? separate thyself, I pray thee, from me.
speaker: Abram
interpretation_notes: Practical conflict-resolution instruction.

full_scripture: Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Part of reaffirmation of Abrahamic promise.

full_scripture: Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it; for I will give it unto thee.
speaker: God
conditions: for I will give it unto thee
interpretation_notes: Symbolic possession and covenant inheritance act.

full_scripture: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine: and he was the priest of the most high God.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Melchizedek ministers as priest of God
interpretation_notes: Introduces Melchizedek as priest-king figure later used typologically in Psalms and Hebrews.

full_scripture: And he blessed him, and said, Blessed be Abram of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth:
speaker: Melchizedek
interpretation_notes: Affirms Abram's covenant relationship with the Most High God.

full_scripture: And blessed be the most high God, which hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand.
speaker: Melchizedek
command_summary: God is blessed for delivering Abram's enemies
interpretation_notes: Attributes military victory directly to divine intervention.

full_scripture: And he gave him tithes of all.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Abram gives a tithe to Melchizedek
interpretation_notes: Not a command but frequently interpreted as a precedent for tithing.

full_scripture: And the king of Sodom said unto Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods to thyself.
speaker: King of Sodom
command_summary: Return the people and keep the goods
interpretation_notes: Political negotiation following Abram's military victory.

full_scripture: And Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lift up mine hand unto the LORD, the most high God, the possessor of heaven and earth, That I will not take from a thread even to a shoelatchet.
speaker: Abram
command_summary: Abram refuses to take spoils from Sodom
interpretation_notes: Abram separates himself from dependence upon the king of Sodom.

full_scripture: Fear not, Abram: I am thy shield, and thy exceeding great reward.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct divine imperative to Abram functioning as covenant reassurance.

full_scripture: Look now toward heaven, and tell the stars, if thou be able to number them.
speaker: God
conditions: if thou be able to number them
command_summary: Look toward heaven and count the stars
interpretation_notes: Imperative functions symbolically to illustrate covenant offspring.

full_scripture: Take me an heifer of three years old, and a she goat of three years old, and a ram of three years old, and a turtledove, and a young pigeon.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Foundational covenant-sacrifice preparation preceding formal covenant ratification.

full_scripture: Know of a surety that thy seed shall be a stranger in a land that is not theirs, and shall serve them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Know the future affliction of your descendants
interpretation_notes: Imperative phrase functions prophetically rather than behaviorally.

full_scripture: Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Euphrates.
speaker: God
command_summary: God grants the promised land to Abram's descendants
interpretation_notes: Formal covenant land grant establishing territorial promise.

full_scripture: And Sarai said unto Abram, Behold now, the LORD hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee, go in unto my maid.
speaker: Sarai
interpretation_notes: Explicit relational instruction from Sarai to Abram intended to obtain children through Hagar.

full_scripture: But Abram said unto Sarai, Behold, thy maid is in thy hand; do to her as it pleaseth thee.
speaker: Abram
conditions: after conflict between Sarai and Hagar
command_summary: Do to Hagar as you see fit
interpretation_notes: Abram delegates authority over Hagar back to Sarai.

full_scripture: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Return to thy mistress, and submit thyself under her hands.
speaker: Angel of the LORD
conditions: after Hagar fled into the wilderness
interpretation_notes: Direct divine instruction mediated through the Angel of the LORD.

full_scripture: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, that it shall not be numbered for multitude.
speaker: Angel of the LORD
interpretation_notes: Divine promise parallel to covenant multiplication themes given elsewhere to Abraham.

full_scripture: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Behold, thou art with child, and shalt bear a son, and shalt call his name Ishmael.
speaker: Angel of the LORD
interpretation_notes: Direct naming command associated with divine revelation.

full_scripture: And he will be a wild man; his hand will be against every man, and every man's hand against him.
speaker: Angel of the LORD
interpretation_notes: Prophetic declaration regarding Ishmael's descendants and future hostility.

full_scripture: I am the Almighty God; walk before me, and be thou perfect.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct covenantal command to Abraham emphasizing covenant faithfulness and moral integrity.

full_scripture: And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Formal reaffirmation of the Abrahamic covenant promises.

full_scripture: As for me, behold, my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.
speaker: God
command_summary: Abraham will become father of many nations
interpretation_notes: Central covenant identity declaration establishing Abraham's expanded covenant role.

full_scripture: Thou shalt keep my covenant therefore, thou, and thy seed after thee in their generations.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Establishes covenant continuity through Abraham's descendants.

full_scripture: Every man child among you shall be circumcised.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Primary covenant sign command within the Abrahamic covenant.

full_scripture: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin; and it shall be a token of the covenant betwixt me and you.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Defines circumcision as covenant marker and identity sign.

full_scripture: And the uncircumcised man child... shall be cut off from his people; he hath broken my covenant.
speaker: God
command_summary: Uncircumcised covenant members are cut off
interpretation_notes: Judicial covenant consequence tied to covenant sign rejection.

full_scripture: And I will fetch a morsel of bread, and comfort ye your hearts; after that ye shall pass on.
speaker: Abraham
interpretation_notes: Hospitality instruction from Abraham to visitors; imperative but culturally contextual.

full_scripture: Make ready quickly three measures of fine meal, knead it, and make cakes upon the hearth.
speaker: Abraham
interpretation_notes: Direct imperative given by Abraham to Sarah during hospitality service.

full_scripture: Dress it.
speaker: Abraham
interpretation_notes: Abraham instructs servant to prepare food for guests.

full_scripture: Where is Sarah thy wife?
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Interrogative speech introducing covenant promise context.

full_scripture: Sarah thy wife shall have a son.
speaker: God
conditions: according to the time of life
interpretation_notes: Core Abrahamic covenant promise reaffirmed.

full_scripture: Is any thing too hard for the LORD?
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Rhetorical question functioning as theological affirmation.

full_scripture: Keep the way of the LORD, to do justice and judgment.
speaker: God
command_summary: Keep the way of the Lord
interpretation_notes: Foundational covenant ethic connecting righteousness and justice.

full_scripture: Hast thou here any besides? son in law, and thy sons, and thy daughters... bring them out of this place:
speaker: Angel
command_summary: Bring your family out of the city
interpretation_notes: Explicit evacuation command preceding divine judgment.

full_scripture: Arise, take thy wife, and thy two daughters, which are here; lest thou be consumed in the iniquity of the city.
speaker: Angel
interpretation_notes: Direct warning command connected to imminent destruction.

full_scripture: Escape for thy life; look not behind thee, neither stay thou in all the plain; escape to the mountain, lest thou be consumed.
speaker: Angel
command_summary: Escape and do not look back
interpretation_notes: Contains multiple imperative clauses forming a unified escape command.

full_scripture: look not behind thee
speaker: Angel
interpretation_notes: Specific prohibition embedded within larger evacuation sequence.

full_scripture: escape to the mountain
speaker: Angel
interpretation_notes: Destination-specific instruction within divine rescue narrative.

full_scripture: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, Behold, thou art but a dead man, for the woman which thou hast taken; for she is a man's wife.
speaker: God
command_summary: Abimelech is under judgment for taking Sarah
interpretation_notes: Direct divine warning to Abimelech regarding Sarah.

full_scripture: Lord, wilt thou slay also a righteous nation?
speaker: Abimelech
interpretation_notes: Interrogative appeal concerning collective justice.

full_scripture: Yea, I know that thou didst this in the integrity of thy heart; for I also withheld thee from sinning against me.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: God explains divine intervention preventing adultery.

full_scripture: Now therefore restore the man his wife; for he is a prophet, and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Explicit divine command with attached consequence and mediation.

full_scripture: and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Divine promise of healing/intercession through Abraham.

full_scripture: and if thou restore her not, know thou that thou shalt surely die, thou, and all that are thine.
speaker: God
command_summary: Failure to restore Sarah brings death
interpretation_notes: Conditional covenant-protection warning.

full_scripture: Therefore Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and told all these things in their ears.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Abimelech reports God's warning to his servants
interpretation_notes: Narrative reporting of leadership response.

full_scripture: So Abraham prayed unto God: and God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maidservants; and they bare children.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Abraham intercedes and God heals Abimelech's household
interpretation_notes: Narrative fulfillment of God's promise regarding intercession.

full_scripture: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac being eight days old, as God had commanded him.
speaker: Narrator
conditions: when male child is eight days old
command_summary: Circumcise male children on the eighth day
interpretation_notes: Narrative obedience referring back to earlier Abrahamic covenant command.

full_scripture: Cast out this bondwoman and her son.
speaker: Sarah
interpretation_notes: Human directive later affirmed partially by divine instruction to Abraham.

full_scripture: In all that Sarah hath said unto thee, hearken unto her voice.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Divine confirmation concerning covenant lineage through Isaac.

full_scripture: And also of the son of the bondwoman will I make a nation, because he is thy seed.
speaker: God
command_summary: God promises to make Ishmael a nation
interpretation_notes: Continuation of prior covenantal promises concerning Ishmael.

full_scripture: Take now thy son, thine only son Isaac, whom thou lovest, and get thee into the land of Moriah.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Beginning of Abraham's testing narrative.

full_scripture: Offer him there for a burnt offering upon one of the mountains which I will tell thee of.
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer Isaac as a burnt offering
interpretation_notes: Testing command central to Abrahamic typology and sacrificial theology.

full_scripture: Lay not thine hand upon the lad, neither do thou any thing unto him.
speaker: Angel of the LORD
interpretation_notes: Divine intervention halting sacrifice after obedience demonstrated.

full_scripture: And Abraham went and took the ram, and offered him up for a burnt offering in the stead of his son.
speaker: Narrator
interpretation_notes: Not directly commanded in text but implied through divine provision.

full_scripture: By myself have I sworn, saith the LORD... in blessing I will bless thee, and in multiplying I will multiply thy seed.
speaker: God
command_summary: God renews covenant blessing after obedience
interpretation_notes: Major Abrahamic covenant reaffirmation linked to obedience.

full_scripture: Give me a possession of a buryingplace with you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.
speaker: Abraham
interpretation_notes: Formal request rather than divine command.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not take a wife unto my son of the daughters of the Canaanites... But thou shalt go unto my country, and to my kindred, and take a wife unto my son Isaac.
speaker: Abraham
command_summary: Do not take a Canaanite wife for Isaac
interpretation_notes: Explicit patriarchal command tied to covenant lineage preservation.

full_scripture: But thou shalt go unto my country, and to my kindred, and take a wife unto my son Isaac.
speaker: Abraham
command_summary: Find a wife for Isaac from Abraham's kindred
interpretation_notes: Positive covenant-preserving marriage instruction.

full_scripture: Beware thou that thou bring not my son thither again.
speaker: Abraham
command_summary: Do not take Isaac back to Mesopotamia
interpretation_notes: Connected to Abrahamic land promise theology.

full_scripture: Only bring not my son thither again.
speaker: Abraham
conditions: if woman will not follow servant
command_summary: Do not return Isaac to the old land
interpretation_notes: Near-direct repetition of Genesis 24:6.

full_scripture: Blessed be the LORD God of my master Abraham.
speaker: Abrahams_servant
interpretation_notes: Doxological blessing rather than imperative command.

full_scripture: Tell me: and if not, tell me.
speaker: Abrahams_servant
interpretation_notes: Narrative negotiation request.

full_scripture: Wilt thou go with this man?
speaker: Rebekahs_family
command_summary: Ask Rebekah whether she will go
interpretation_notes: Interrogative form but functionally a decision directive.

full_scripture: Go not down into Egypt; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct divine prohibition to Isaac during famine.

full_scripture: Dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Continuation of Abrahamic covenant geography.

full_scripture: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with thee, and will bless thee.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Contains both command and covenant promise.

full_scripture: Because that Abraham obeyed my voice, and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Retrospective covenant-faithfulness statement.

full_scripture: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.
speaker: Rebekah
interpretation_notes: Human instruction tied to Jacob obtaining blessing.

full_scripture: Go now to the flock, and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goats.
speaker: Rebekah
interpretation_notes: Part of blessing acquisition narrative.

full_scripture: Only obey my voice, and go fetch me them.
speaker: Rebekah
interpretation_notes: Repetition/intensification of Rebekah's instruction.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan. Arise, go to Padanaram, to the house of Bethuel thy mother's father; and take thee a wife from thence of the daughters of Laban.
speaker: Isaac
command_summary: Do not marry a Canaanite woman
interpretation_notes: Continuation of covenant-separation marriage pattern established in patriarch narratives.

full_scripture: Arise, go to Padanaram, to the house of Bethuel thy mother's father.
speaker: Isaac
interpretation_notes: Migration instruction connected to covenant lineage preservation.

full_scripture: Take thee a wife from thence of the daughters of Laban.
speaker: Isaac
command_summary: Take a wife from Laban's household
interpretation_notes: Specific covenant-family marriage directive.

full_scripture: The land whereon thou liest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct continuation of Abrahamic covenant promises.

full_scripture: Behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Foundational divine-presence promise recurring throughout scripture.

full_scripture: Abide with me.
speaker: Laban
interpretation_notes: Simple narrative imperative with limited theological weight.

full_scripture: Tarry: for I have learned by experience that the LORD hath blessed me for thy sake. Appoint me thy wages.
speaker: Laban
interpretation_notes: Economic negotiation instruction within patriarchal labor arrangement.

full_scripture: Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Direct divine instruction to Jacob tied to covenant continuity.

full_scripture: Arise, get thee out from this land, and return unto the land of thy kindred.
speaker: God
command_summary: Leave the land and return home
interpretation_notes: Restatement and intensification of God's earlier command to Jacob.

full_scripture: Take heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not threaten or manipulate Jacob
interpretation_notes: Hebrew idiom likely forbids harmful interference rather than all speech.

full_scripture: Come now therefore, let us make a covenant, I and thou.
speaker: Laban
interpretation_notes: Human covenant proposal rather than divine ordinance.

full_scripture: Return unto thy country, and to thy kindred, and I will deal well with thee.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Jacob recounts and appeals to prior divine instruction.

full_scripture: Let me go, for the day breaketh.
speaker: Angel
interpretation_notes: Command occurs within mysterious divine encounter narrative.

full_scripture: Who are those with thee?
speaker: Esau
interpretation_notes: Included for conversational structure completeness though not a command.

full_scripture: The soul of my son Shechem longeth for your daughter: I pray you give her him to wife... and dwell with us.
speaker: Hamor
command_summary: Give Dinah to Shechem as wife
interpretation_notes: Marriage and integration proposal from Hamor to Jacob's family.

full_scripture: If ye will be as we be, that every male of you be circumcised...
speaker: Sons of Jacob
interpretation_notes: Circumcision used deceptively as covenant condition.

full_scripture: Arise, go up to Bethel, and dwell there: and make there an altar unto God.
speaker: God
command_summary: Go to Bethel and build altar
interpretation_notes: Renewal of Jacob's covenant worship obligations.

full_scripture: Put away the strange gods that are among you, and be clean, and change your garments.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Purification before covenant worship at Bethel.

full_scripture: Let us arise, and go up to Bethel; and I will make there an altar unto God.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Corporate covenant movement toward sacred location.

full_scripture: Thy name shall not be called any more Jacob, but Israel shall be thy name.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Covenant identity reaffirmation.

full_scripture: Be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall be of thee.
speaker: God
interpretation_notes: Continuation of creation and Abrahamic covenant blessing.

full_scripture: And Israel said unto Joseph, Do not thy brethren feed the flock in Shechem? come, and I will send thee unto them.
speaker: Israel
command_summary: Go to your brothers in Shechem
interpretation_notes: Direct paternal instruction from Israel to Joseph.

full_scripture: And he said to him, Go, I pray thee, see whether it be well with thy brethren, and well with the flocks.
speaker: Israel
command_summary: Check on your brothers and the flocks
interpretation_notes: Continuation of Israel's instruction to Joseph.

full_scripture: Come now therefore, and let us slay him, and cast him into some pit.
speaker: Sons of Jacob
command_summary: Kill Joseph and throw him into a pit
interpretation_notes: Collective conspiracy instruction by Joseph's brothers.

full_scripture: And Reuben heard it, and he delivered him out of their hands; and said, Let us not kill him.
speaker: Reuben
interpretation_notes: Reuben attempts to restrain the murder plot.

full_scripture: And Reuben said unto them, Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness.
speaker: Reuben
interpretation_notes: Reuben redirects the brothers away from murder.

full_scripture: And Judah said unto Onan, Go in unto thy brother's wife, and marry her, and raise up seed to thy brother.
speaker: Judah
command_summary: Marry your brother's wife and raise offspring
interpretation_notes: Levirate-style familial obligation before Mosaic codification.

full_scripture: Then said Judah to Tamar his daughter in law, Remain a widow at thy father's house.
speaker: Judah
command_summary: Remain a widow in your father's house
interpretation_notes: Temporary familial instruction from Judah to Tamar.

full_scripture: And she said, Lie with me.
speaker: Potiphars_wife
interpretation_notes: Direct sexual temptation toward Joseph.

full_scripture: How then can I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?
speaker: Joseph
command_summary: Do not commit sexual sin against God
interpretation_notes: Expressed as rhetorical refusal but carries strong inferred moral principle.

full_scripture: Do not interpretations belong to God? tell me them, I pray you.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph attributes interpretive authority to God while requesting disclosure of dreams.

full_scripture: But think on me when it shall be well with thee.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph requests covenant-like remembrance from the butler.

full_scripture: and shew kindness, I pray thee, unto me.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Appeal for covenant loyalty and mercy.

full_scripture: and make mention of me unto Pharaoh.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Direct appeal for advocacy before governing authority.

full_scripture: and bring me out of this house:
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph seeks liberation from unjust imprisonment.

full_scripture: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph.
speaker: Pharaoh
interpretation_notes: Royal administrative summons.

full_scripture: Now therefore let Pharaoh look out a man discreet and wise.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph transitions from dream interpretation into administrative counsel.

full_scripture: Let Pharaoh do this, and let him appoint officers over the land.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Administrative structure for famine response.

full_scripture: And let them gather all the food of those good years that come.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Central stewardship strategy for preservation during famine.

full_scripture: Get you down thither, and buy for us from thence.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Go to Egypt and buy grain
interpretation_notes: Family preservation command during regional famine.

full_scripture: The man did solemnly protest unto us, saying, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
speaker: Judah
conditions: except your brother be with you
interpretation_notes: Joseph's condition relayed through Judah.

full_scripture: If thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food.
speaker: Judah
interpretation_notes: Conditional proposal tied to survival.

full_scripture: But if thou wilt not send him, we will not go down.
speaker: Judah
command_summary: We will not travel without Benjamin
interpretation_notes: Restates Joseph's condition through refusal.

full_scripture: Take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a present.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Gift diplomacy for reconciliation.

full_scripture: And take double money in your hand.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Repayment and provision planning.

full_scripture: Take also your brother, and arise, go again unto the man.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Bring Benjamin and return to Egypt
interpretation_notes: Jacob finally permits Benjamin to travel.

full_scripture: And God Almighty give you mercy before the man.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Prayerful blessing rather than direct command.

full_scripture: Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph directs household management.

full_scripture: And put my cup, the silver cup, in the sack's mouth of the youngest.
speaker: Joseph
command_summary: Place the silver cup in Benjamin's sack
interpretation_notes: Joseph's loyalty test setup.

full_scripture: Up, follow after the men.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph advances the test sequence.

full_scripture: Is not this it in which my lord drinketh?
speaker: Josephs_Steward
interpretation_notes: Interrogative accusation during Joseph's test.

full_scripture: Come near to me, I pray you.
speaker: Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph begins reconciliation.

full_scripture: Now therefore be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither.
speaker: Joseph
command_summary: Do not grieve over selling Joseph
interpretation_notes: Joseph reframes suffering through providence.

full_scripture: I am God, the God of thy father: fear not to go down into Egypt;
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not fear going to Egypt
interpretation_notes: Direct divine reassurance to Jacob during covenant transition into Egypt.

full_scripture: for I will there make of thee a great nation:
speaker: God
command_summary: God will make Israel a great nation in Egypt
interpretation_notes: Continuation of Abrahamic covenant promises through Jacob.

full_scripture: I will go down with thee into Egypt; and I will also surely bring thee up again:
speaker: God
command_summary: God promises presence and future return
interpretation_notes: Foreshadows eventual Exodus restoration.

full_scripture: When Pharaoh shall call you, and shall say, What is your occupation? That ye shall say, Thy servants' trade hath been about cattle from our youth even until now.
speaker: Joseph
command_summary: Tell Pharaoh you are keepers of cattle
interpretation_notes: Strategic instruction intended to secure settlement in Goshen.

full_scripture: the land of Egypt is before thee; in the best of the land make thy father and brethren to dwell;
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Settle your family in the best land
interpretation_notes: Administrative provision granted through Joseph's favor.

full_scripture: and if thou knowest any men of activity among them, then make them rulers over my cattle.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Appoint capable men over Pharaoh's cattle
interpretation_notes: Delegated administrative authority under Egyptian governance.

full_scripture: bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt:
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Do not bury Jacob in Egypt
interpretation_notes: Expresses covenantal attachment to promised land.

full_scripture: but I will lie with my fathers, and thou shalt carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their buryingplace.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Carry Jacob out of Egypt for burial
interpretation_notes: Burial in Canaan symbolizes covenant continuity.

full_scripture: God Almighty appeared unto me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessed me, And said unto me, Behold, I will make thee fruitful, and multiply thee.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Reaffirmation of Abrahamic covenant promises to Jacob.

full_scripture: Bring them, I pray thee, unto me, and I will bless them.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Bring Ephraim and Manasseh for blessing
interpretation_notes: Formal patriarchal blessing transfer.

full_scripture: In thee shall Israel bless, saying, God make thee as Ephraim and as Manasseh.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Future blessing formula established through Ephraim and Manasseh
interpretation_notes: Prophetic covenantal blessing formula.

full_scripture: Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the last days.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Gather for prophetic blessing and prophecy
interpretation_notes: Introduction to tribal prophetic declarations.

full_scripture: Gather yourselves together, and hear, ye sons of Jacob; and hearken unto Israel your father.
speaker: Jacob
command_summary: Hear the words of Israel your father
interpretation_notes: Parallel summons reinforcing prophetic authority.

full_scripture: Bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite.
speaker: Jacob
interpretation_notes: Burial request tied to covenant land identity.

full_scripture: My father made me swear, saying, Lo, I die: in my grave which I have digged for me in the land of Canaan, there shalt thou bury me.
speaker: Joseph
command_summary: Joseph must bury Jacob in Canaan
interpretation_notes: Joseph recounts Jacob's covenant burial command to Pharaoh.

full_scripture: God will surely visit you, and bring you out of this land unto the land which he sware to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
speaker: Joseph
command_summary: God will bring Israel back to the promised land
interpretation_notes: Forward-looking covenant promise anticipating Exodus.

full_scripture: God will surely visit you, and ye shall carry up my bones from hence.
speaker: Joseph
conditions: when God brings Israel out of Egypt
interpretation_notes: Burial instruction rooted in covenant expectation of return.
